initiative: US banks reject CLARITY Act stablecoin deal days before Senate vote

initiative: Coinbase x402 powers Amazon’s AI agent payments
description: AWS embedded Coinbase's x402 payment protocol and wallet infrastructure directly into Amazon Bedrock AgentCore on May 7, enabling autonomous AI agents to transact in USDC on the Base network without human authorization. Settlement occurs in approximately 200 milliseconds at sub-cent cost, with agents never holding private keys — authentication, signing, and execution are handled through a single API call that incorporates enterprise spending controls and compliance checks. The x402 protocol, an open HTTP-native standard built on the existing "402 Payment Required" status code, has already processed over 169 million payments in its first year, with AWS and Coinbase serving as co-founding members of the x402 Foundation alongside Cloudflare.

initiative: Morgan Stanley’s ETrade crypto undercuts Coinbase

The pricing undercuts Charles Schwab's recently launched 75bps offering, Fidelity's approximately 100bps fee, and most Coinbase retail tiers, setting up a direct fee compression dynamic across the US retail brokerage market. The service grants clients direct asset ownership rather than fund-wrapper exposure and is slated to expand to all 8.6 million E*Trade users later in 2026, alongside a proprietary digital wallet intended to hold crypto alongside tokenized securities.

initiative: US Senate Banking Committee Expected to Hold a Formal Vote on CLARITY Act as Early as Next Week
description: The Senate Banking Committee is expected to hold a markup vote on the Digital Asset Market CLARITY Act as early as the week of May 11, with draft legislative text already circulated to select industry participants. The bill, which passed the House 294–134 in July 2025, would draw a statutory boundary between SEC and CFTC jurisdiction over digital assets, assigning digital commodities to the CFTC while leaving digital securities under SEC oversight. Coinbase VP for US Policy Kara Calvert, speaking at Consensus 2026, emphasized that the bill requires at least 60 Senate votes and therefore depends on bipartisan support—a threshold complicated by Democratic demands for a token-trading ban on lawmakers and outstanding Republican holdouts.

initiative: Meta’s USDC pilot draws fire as Senator Warren demands stablecoin transparency
description: Meta quietly began testing USDC payouts for content creators in Colombia and the Philippines in late April, routing earnings through Circle's dollar-pegged stablecoin on the Solana and Polygon networks via Stripe's settlement infrastructure, rather than through traditional fiat rails. Senator Elizabeth Warren responded by writing directly to CEO Mark Zuckerberg, citing the company's "lack of transparency" and demanding answers by May 20 on which stablecoins and wallets are involved, how issuers are selected, what user data is collected, and what discussions, if any, have taken place with the Federal Reserve, SEC, CFTC, and banking regulators.

initiative: SEC chair Atkins signals new rules for onchain markets, AI-driven finance
description: SEC Chair Paul Atkins used a public appearance at the AI+ Expo in Washington to formally signal that the agency intends to pursue notice-and-comment rulemaking — rather than continued enforcement actions — to address how existing securities statutes apply to blockchain-based trading, settlement infrastructure, crypto vaults, and AI-driven financial applications. The core regulatory problem he identified is structural: securities law was designed around discrete intermediaries such as brokers, exchanges, and clearinghouses, whereas modern blockchain protocols can bundle all of those functions into a single piece of software, creating ambiguity about which rules apply and to whom.

The deal gives Bullish direct control over the ownership registries, legal registrations, and operational history that tokenized equity issuance requires at institutional scale — infrastructure that competitors such as Securitize and Ondo Finance would need years to replicate organically. The transaction arrives as the regulatory framework for tokenized securities solidifies, following the SEC's March 2026 approval of Nasdaq's tokenized stock trading pilot and Federal Reserve guidance on how banks should account for tokenized securities.

description: MoonPay, primarily known as a fiat-to-crypto on-ramp, has completed the acquisition of DFlow, a Solana-based order routing and execution infrastructure provider, in an all-stock transaction reported at approximately $100 million. DFlow had already established itself as embedded trading infrastructure for major counterparties including Coinbase and Phantom, processing roughly 10 million transactions per month and over $50 billion in cumulative volume across Solana. The deal extends MoonPay's addressable surface beyond retail conversion flows into the execution layer, positioning it to serve high-frequency trading pipelines and programmatic or AI-agent-driven transaction flows.

initiative: Garlinghouse defends Clarity Act Senate momentum
description: Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse used his appearance at Consensus 2026 in Miami to publicly back the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act, citing growing Senate support as evidence that meaningful legislative progress is underway. The bill has attracted endorsements from Coinbase and Circle, which urged the Senate Banking Committee to advance it following a stablecoin yield compromise negotiated by Senators Tillis and Alsobrooks, while major US banking associations have opposed those yield provisions on systemic risk grounds. SEC Chair Paul Atkins and CFTC Chair Brian Selig were both present at the conference, underscoring the elevated regulatory attention surrounding the legislation.

initiative: Ripple to share DPRK hacker intel with crypto industry after $577M in DeFi hacks
description: Ripple has begun contributing proprietary threat intelligence on North Korean hacking activity to Crypto ISAC, a not-for-profit information-sharing collective focused on the digital asset sector, providing members with enriched profiles of suspected DPRK IT operatives and detailed indicators of compromise drawn from active campaigns. The move follows an estimated $577 million in losses attributed to North Korea–linked actors in 2026 alone, concentrated in two DeFi exploits — a six-month social engineering operation against Solana-based Drift Protocol that drained $285 million and a coordinated RPC node compromise of KelpDAO that netted roughly $292 million — representing 76% of all crypto hack losses year-to-date.

initiative: Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse warns next two weeks are critical for crypto legislation
description: With the House having already passed its version of broad digital asset market structure legislation (the "Clarity Act") last year, the Senate remains the critical bottleneck, requiring passage out of both the Agriculture and Banking committees before a bill can reach the president's desk. The Agriculture Committee has cleared its version, but the Banking Committee has stalled over disagreements on stablecoin yield treatment—a logjam that Senators Alsobrooks and Tillis partially addressed with a compromise reached last week, potentially enabling a markup vote this month.

initiative: Google Cloud, Solana Foundation roll out new pay-as-you-go system for AI agents
description: The Solana Foundation and Google Cloud jointly launched Pay.sh, an open payments infrastructure that allows AI agents to access and pay for API services—including Google Cloud's own BigQuery, Gemini, and Cloud Run—using stablecoins on Solana, without requiring account creation or subscriptions. The system operates via an API proxy hosted on Google Cloud Platform that authenticates agent requests and routes payments through either Coinbase's x402 protocol or Stripe/Tempo's Machine Payments Protocol, giving it interoperability with two competing emerging standards for autonomous machine commerce.

initiative: Santiago Bausili: Finance, economics and investments
description: Central bank speech | 05 May 2026 by Santiago Bausili PDF full text (14kb) | 6 pages The objectives set by the stabilization program that we started implementing at the end of 2023 are in line with the objectives of a traditional stabilization program: on the one hand, to reduce inflation and anchor expectations of the evolution about prices; and on the other, to stabilize the exchange rate. Interest rates and interest rate volatility declined significantly; inflation expectations remained anchored on a downward path; the exchange rate remained stable; and the BCRA purchased more reserves than anticipated by the market.

initiative: Sanjay Malhotra: Indian financial markets - resilience and resurgence
description: Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra delivered the keynote address at the 25th FIMMDA-PDAI Annual Conference in Amsterdam, setting out both the current state of Indian financial markets and the RBI's policy agenda for their further development. Speaking against a backdrop of global stress — including geo-economic fragmentation from tariff escalation, stretched asset valuations, growing private credit opacity, and renewed geopolitical tension in West Asia — Malhotra argued that India's macroeconomic fundamentals remain robust, citing 7.6% GDP growth in FY2025-26 and headline CPI inflation running below the 4% target.

initiative: Chiara Scotti: Digital money and the architecture of trust
description: The central question, therefore, is not whether central bank digital currencies (CBDCs), stablecoins or tokenized deposits can each, individually, perform the role that traditional forms of money have historically played. The more relevant question is what kind of institutional architecture combining different settlement assets, institutions and infrastructures can deliver trust, resilience and effective monetary control while at the same time accompanying innovation, without hindering it.

initiative: Piero Cipollone: Digital assets, payment efficiency and monetary policy
description: ECB Executive Board member Piero Cipollone delivered a speech at a joint ECB, Bank of Italy, and CEPR workshop in Rome on 4 May 2026, setting out the Eurosystem's strategic rationale for issuing tokenised central bank money and expanding collateral eligibility to DLT-based assets. Cipollone argued that tokenisation qualifies as a general-purpose technology requiring coordinated adoption across the financial system, and that without a public settlement anchor, tokenised markets would be forced to rely on private instruments—most likely stablecoins—at a scale that could impair monetary policy transmission, introduce run risk, and erode monetary sovereignty.

initiative: Elon Musk settles SEC Twitter case with $1.5M fine
description: Elon Musk reached a civil settlement with the SEC on May 4, 2026, resolving allegations that he filed a required beneficial ownership disclosure 11 days late when accumulating a stake exceeding 5% in Twitter ahead of his $44 billion acquisition in 2022. The regulator contended the delayed filing allowed Musk to purchase additional shares at lower prices before the market was aware of his position, generating an estimated $150 million in avoided costs — an amount he will not be required to disgorge under the terms of the agreement. A trust in Musk's name will pay a $1.5 million civil penalty without admitting wrongdoing, subject to court approval.

initiative: ESMA consults on a new simplified approach to updating MMF stress test parameters
description: ESMA has opened a public consultation proposing to replace the current process of annually amending Section 5 of its Money Market Fund stress testing Guidelines with a streamlined approach under which updated calibration parameters would instead be published each year on a dedicated webpage. Under the proposal, the Guidelines themselves would remain in place to govern the overarching stress testing framework and methodology, while the website would become the single authoritative source for the latest parameter calibrations. The change is designed to allow market participants to apply revised parameters immediately upon approval, rather than waiting for a formal guidelines amendment, and forms part of ESMA's broader Simplification and Burden Reduction initiative.

initiative: Ripple shares North Korea-linked threat data as crypto attacks shift to social engineering
description: Ripple has contributed its proprietary threat intelligence — including fraud-associated domains, wallet addresses, and indicators of compromise tied to North Korean hacking campaigns — to Crypto ISAC, a sector-focused information-sharing nonprofit, which simultaneously launched a new API enabling real-time data distribution among members including Coinbase. The disclosure is framed around a documented tactical shift by North Korea-linked actors away from on-chain exploits toward insider-style social engineering, as illustrated by the $280 million Drift incident, where attackers gained the trust of contributors and compromised their devices rather than targeting smart contract vulnerabilities.

SMSFs are regulated directly by the Australian Taxation Office and collectively held approximately AU$1.06 trillion across roughly 664,000 funds at end-2025, representing a substantial addressable pool of retirement capital that is already legally permitted to hold crypto.

The Dallas-based firm has added more than 2,200 BTC since its January 2026 acquisition of Semler Scientific, financing accumulation in part through its SATA preferred stock instrument, which raised $225 million against more than $600 million in demand at a roughly 13% annualized yield. Strive also carries a $50.4 million allocation to Strategy-linked preferred stock, creating cross-exposure to Michael Saylor's larger treasury structure, which holds 818,334 BTC at an average cost of $75,537.

initiative: Moscow Exchange bets bigger on crypto with SOL, XRP, TRX and BNB indexes

The exchange also plans to increase the update frequency of its existing Bitcoin and Ethereum benchmarks to every 15 seconds during trading hours and weekend sessions. The indexes are not yet designated as underlying assets for tradable products, though the exchange indicated they could serve as the basis for future instruments, consistent with Russia's model of restricting crypto-linked exposure to qualified investors through regulated derivatives rather than spot markets.

initiative: Bank groups challenge Senator Thom Tillis over stablecoin proposal

The groups cited economist Andrew Nigrinis's analysis warning that broad stablecoin adoption could reduce consumer, small-business, and farm lending by one-fifth or more, a materially different picture from White House estimates suggesting only a $2.1 billion uplift in bank lending from a yield ban.

initiative: Coinbase says crypto bill deal clears Senate path
description: Senate negotiators Thom Tillis and Angela Alsobrooks reached a compromise on the stablecoin rewards provision that had been blocking progress on the CLARITY Act, the U.S. digital asset market structure bill. Under the agreed language, rewards that are economically equivalent to bank deposit interest would be prohibited, addressing banking industry concerns that yield-bearing stablecoin products could draw deposits away from traditional lenders, while activity-based user rewards tied to platform usage would remain permissible. Coinbase, which lobbied to preserve the activity-based carve-out, confirmed the deal through its Chief Policy Officer, noting that regulators will still need to define the boundary between allowed and prohibited reward structures.

Cred filed for bankruptcy in late 2020, leaving affected users with unrecovered digital asset losses, and the settlement amount exceeds five times the fees Uphold collected from the arrangement.

description: Tether now counts roughly $20 billion in physical gold and about $7 billion in bitcoin among its reserves, as stablecoins see growing use in international payments. initiative: SBI Holdings eyes stake in crypto exchange Bitbank to build digital asset powerhouse
description: SBI Holdings has submitted a letter of intent to acquire a stake in Bitbank Co., Ltd.—one of Japan's largest retail crypto exchanges—with the explicit intention of consolidating it as a subsidiary, extending a domestic roll-up strategy that already includes the full absorption of regulated exchange Bitpoint. The move is timed against a material regulatory inflection point: Japan's cabinet has approved a draft amendment that would bring crypto assets under the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act, the same framework governing equities and securities, potentially effective fiscal 2027, which would significantly raise compliance burdens and favor well-capitalized, institutionally affiliated platforms.

initiative: A16z backs CFTC, says state-by-state rules on prediction markets create ‘barrier to impartial access’
description: Andreessen Horowitz submitted an 18-page comment letter to the CFTC arguing that state-level enforcement actions — including cease-and-desist orders and proposed bans issued by regulators in Illinois, Arizona, Connecticut, New York, and Wisconsin — undermine the commission's exclusive federal jurisdiction over event contracts classified as swaps. The firm contended that compelling exchanges to geo-block users by state of residence would fragment liquidity and violate CFTC fair-access requirements, effectively disadvantaging retail and institutional participants in states pursuing their own licensing or prohibition regimes.

initiative: European crypto asset manager CoinShares records $7.4 billion AUM in first annual filing since Nasdaq listing
description: CoinShares filed its first U.S. annual report following the completion of a $1.2 billion SPAC merger with Vine Hill, through which it began trading on Nasdaq under the ticker CSHR earlier in May 2026, adding a U.S. listing to its existing European public status. The filing disclosed $7.4 billion in gross AUM and $165.7 million in total full-year revenue for 2025, with asset management revenue growing 13% year-on-year to $126.4 million, while reported net income declined to $114.3 million largely due to the absence of a one-time $36.8 million FTX claims gain that inflated 2024 figures.

description: Cumulatively, assets under management for the firm's crypto ETPs rose to roughly $1.8 billion during Q1 of this year.

initiative: SBI Holdings in talks to acquire stake in crypto exchange Bitbank, eyes subsidiary status
description: SBI Holdings has submitted a letter of intent to acquire a controlling stake in Bitbank, one of Japan's larger domestic crypto exchanges, with the goal of making it a consolidated subsidiary — a structurally significant threshold that would bring Bitbank onto SBI's balance sheet and under its regulatory perimeter. The move follows SBI's absorption of Bitpoint Japan into its in-house crypto arm, SBI VC Trade, last month, indicating a deliberate strategy to consolidate Japan's fragmented retail crypto exchange landscape under a single group umbrella ahead of anticipated regulatory tightening as crypto assets move toward coverage under the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act.

initiative: Tiff Macklem: Release of the Monetary Policy Report
description: Bank of Canada Holds Rate at 2.25% Amid Middle East-Driven Energy Shock At its April 29, 2026 decision, the Bank of Canada's Governing Council held the overnight policy rate steady at 2.25% — unchanged since October 2025 — as policymakers weighed competing pressures from an energy-driven inflation spike against softening domestic demand and trade headwinds from U.S. tariffs. The escalation of the Middle East conflict has driven global oil prices to approximately USD 90/barrel, pushing Canadian CPI from 1.8% in February to 2.4% in March, with the Bank projecting a peak near 3% in April before a return to the 2% target by early 2026, contingent on oil prices declining to around USD 75/barrel.

initiative: Aleš Michl: A central bank. And bitcoin.
description: Czech National Bank Governor Aleš Michl delivered a formal speech at The Bitcoin Conference 2026 in Las Vegas, marking a notable instance of a sitting G10-adjacent central bank governor directly engaging a crypto-native audience at an industry event. The speech framed the CNB's post-2022 monetary tightening—which successfully reduced inflation from near 20% to the 2% target—as a philosophical foundation for evaluating Bitcoin alongside traditional reserve management principles. Michl positioned the CNB's approach as structurally hawkish and pro-saving, implicitly signaling openness to hard-asset or scarcity-based monetary concepts consistent with that framework.

initiative: Christodoulos Patsalides: Keynote speech - Eurofi High Level Seminar
description: Keynote speech by Mr Christodoulos Patsalides, Governor of the Central Bank of Cyprus, at the Eurofi High Level Seminar Digital Euro, Nicosia, 27 March 2026.

initiative: Burkhard Balz: Foundations of resilience - the role of cash and the digital euro
description: Keynote speech by Mr Burkhard Balz, Member of the Executive Board of the Deutsche Bundesbank, at the Global Currency Forum (GCF), Antalya, 29 April 2026.

initiative: Jorgovanka Tabaković: Brave old world
description: At an event in Belgrade hosted by the National Bank of Serbia, Governor Jorgovanka Tabaković delivered remarks at the launch of the EBRD's *Transition Report: Brave Old World*, presented by EBRD Research Director Ralph De Haas alongside regional officials including former North Macedonia central bank Governor Dimitar Bogov. The speech used the report's demographic focus as a platform to articulate the NBS's institutional view that declining fertility, labour force contraction, and population ageing represent primary structural risks to GDP growth, fiscal sustainability, and inflation dynamics across both Serbia and the broader European transition region.

initiative: Stablecoins losing cross-border appeal; Circle v Tether in DeFi fight
description: Stablecoins Tether and Circle are locked in a DeFi fight, Meta pilots USDC payouts, and EU sanctions tighten on Russia and digital ruble-linked tokens.

initiative: Philippines: dYdX among 7 ‘crypto’ platforms flagged by SEC
description: The Philippine SEC published a formal investor alert naming dYdX alongside AEVO, GTrade, Pacifica, Orderly, Deriv, and Ostium as unregistered platforms soliciting investments from Filipino residents without the licenses required under the country's Crypto-Asset Service Provider (CASP) Rules, which mandate registration for any entity—domestic or foreign—offering crypto-asset services to persons in the Philippines. None of the seven platforms hold SEC registration or authorization, exposing them and any local promoters to penalties under the Securities Regulation Code of up to PHP 5 million (approximately USD 89,000) and up to 21 years' imprisonment.

initiative: CommBank builds AI agent that spots fraud and helps build defences
description: Commonwealth Bank of Australia has deployed an agentic AI system designed to help detect emerging fraud and scam patterns in transaction and payments data and generate the rules needed to help intercept them.

initiative: PayPal reorganisation gives Venmo its own business unit
description: PayPal is reorganising its business into three units, including one dedicated to Venmo, as new CEO Enrique Lores bids to reinvigorate the payments giant in the face of increasing competition and potential takeover bids.

initiative: Australian regulator warns of ungoverned AI risks to banks
description: The Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (Apra) has called for a step-change in how banks, insurers and superannuation trustees manage AI-related risks as the technology continues to rapidly evolve.

initiative: Ripple opens regional HQ in the UAE
description: Ripple, the leading provider of blockchain-based enterprise solutions across traditional and digital finance, today announced an expansion of its presence in the UAE with the opening of its new Middle East and Africa (MEA) regional headquarters in the Dubai International Financial Centre (DIFC), creating capacity to grow its local team as demand for regulated blockchain-powered payment and custody solutions continues

initiative: Tether Proposes Three-Way Merger to Build Integrated Bitcoin Platform Around Twenty One Capital
description: Tether Investments, the largest shareholder of NYSE-listed Twenty One Capital (ticker: XXI), has formally proposed merging XXI with Strike—the bitcoin payments and financial services firm led by Jack Mallers—and Elektron Energy, a bitcoin mining operator controlling roughly 50 EH/s (approximately 5% of total network hashrate) at sub-$60,000 all-in production costs. The transaction would consolidate a bitcoin treasury vehicle, a retail and institutional lending/payments business, and a large-scale mining operation into a single publicly listed entity, with Tether signaling it will vote its controlling stake in favor of both deals.

initiative: Shifting forces behind RMB internationalization: evidence from the 2025 Triennial Survey
description: Using granular data from the 2025 BIS Triennial Survey on global FX turnover — including previously restricted BIS locational banking statistics and international debt securities data — researchers find that cross-border banking linkages with China, rather than bilateral trade flows, are the primary short-term driver of RMB FX trading growth across jurisdictions. Policy-driven mechanisms such as qualified investor licence programmes and free trade agreements emerge as significant long-term structural determinants, though their effects materialise slowly beyond the three-year survey interval.

initiative: Digitalisation and innovation - opportunities and risks for financial health
description: BIS FSI Brief: Digitalisation, Innovation, and Financial Health Risks The Bank for International Settlements' Financial Stability Institute published a 22-page brief examining how digital financial services — spanning payments, credit, savings, and insurance — are simultaneously improving access and generating new consumer harm vectors, including a documented global surge in scams and fraud, overindebtedness among digital borrowers, and mis-selling of complex investment products. Despite broader digital adoption, aggregate financial health indices are declining in several jurisdictions, undermining the assumed positive correlation between fintech penetration and household financial resilience.

initiative: Scope of Insurers Subject to the Recovery and Resolution Planning Requirements in the FSB Key Attributes: Final Report
description: The Financial Stability Board has published its final guidance establishing a structured framework for national authorities to determine which insurers must be subject to recovery and resolution planning requirements under the FSB's Key Attributes of Effective Resolution Regimes. The report follows the FSB's 2022 decision to discontinue the annual designation of global systemically important insurers, replacing that binary G-SII identification process with a more flexible, criteria-based scoping methodology that individual jurisdictions apply using their own supervisory judgment and market context.

initiative: Scope of Insurers Subject to Recovery and Resolution Planning Requirements in the Key Attributes: Overview of consultation responses
description: The FSB received nine responses to its November 2025 consultation on criteria for determining which insurers should be subject to recovery and resolution planning requirements under the Key Attributes framework, with the consultation period closing in February 2026. Respondents — drawn from both public authorities and private sector participants — broadly supported the proposed scoping criteria but flagged concerns around regulatory overreach and potential duplication with existing national or sectoral frameworks. The FSB has now published both an overview of those responses and a final report incorporating targeted revisions to address the feedback, alongside updated guidance on identifying critical functions for insurers.

initiative: Identification of Critical Functions of Insurers: Practices paper – Revised version
description: The Financial Stability Board has released a revised version of its 2023 practices paper on identifying critical functions of insurers, updated to reflect a new definition of "critical function" introduced in the FSB's concurrently finalised guidance on the scope of insurers subject to recovery and resolution planning under the Key Attributes framework. The revision draws on approaches from four jurisdictions — Australia, China, France, and the Netherlands — documenting how each has developed or advanced methodology for scoping which insurer functions are sufficiently vital to the real economy that their disruption would warrant resolution intervention.

initiative: Key Attributes Assessment Methodology for the Insurance Sector: Revised version
description: The Financial Stability Board published a revised version of its Key Attributes Assessment Methodology for the Insurance Sector on 29 April 2026, updating the 2020 original to incorporate changes stemming from newly finalized guidance on which insurers fall within the scope of recovery and resolution planning requirements. The revision reflects the FSB's 2022 decision—reaffirmed in November 2025—to discontinue annual identification of global systemically important insurers (G-SIIs) and instead rely on the IAIS Holistic Framework to assess systemic risk, a structural shift that necessitated an updated definition of "critical function" embedded in the methodology via an explanatory note.

initiative: Developing Effective Resolution Strategies and Plans for Systemically Important Insurers: Revised version
description: The Financial Stability Board has released a revised version of its 2016 guidance on resolution strategies and plans for systemically important insurers, updated to incorporate changes to the definition of "critical functions" introduced through a companion scoping paper finalized simultaneously. The revised guidance is directed at national resolution authorities and Crisis Management Groups overseeing global systemically important insurers, setting out how preferred resolution strategies should be determined by analyzing business models, policyholder protection arrangements, and the criticality of specific insurance functions.

initiative: Singapore proposes lower-risk rules for some digital assets
description: Singapore proposes tailored crypto rules, giving tokenized assets and stablecoins lower-risk capital treatment under updated central bank's consultation.

initiative: Australia’s Crypto Crossroads: Regulation is Here, Now Comes the Hard Part
description: Macropod’s AUDM, Australia’s first AUD-backed stablecoin issued under an AFSL and a participant in the Reserve Bank’s Project Acacia, is now listed on major domestic exchanges with compliance and monitoring infrastructure embedded from the outset, including Chainalysis tools for entity risk scoring and real-time token tracking. Stablecoin oversight and scam prevention are being developed on separate policy tracks — stablecoins through payments reform legislation, scams through the Scam Prevention Framework targeting banks, telcos, and social media platforms.

initiative: Mugur Isărescu: South-east Europe's next leap forward
description: Speech by Mr Mugur Isărescu, Governor of the National Bank of Romania, at the Economist Impact event: Romania Government Roundtable, Bucharest, 30 March 2026.

initiative: Chiara Scotti: From analysis to action - AI in financial markets
description: The Bank of Italy's Deputy Governor Chiara Scotti delivered closing remarks at a trilateral event co-organized by the OECD, European Commission, and Banca d'Italia to present a joint report examining how artificial intelligence is being adopted across Italian financial markets. The report maps current AI deployment practices among Italian market participants, benchmarks them against cross-country experience, and identifies regulatory gaps where greater clarity, coordination, and supervisory capacity are needed.
